title: Dirty Worktree Contract Sweep
description: Use .workflow/issues/discoveries/DBP-20260609-033400/dirty-files.txt. Scan current uncommitted modified product files for introduced contract, schema, snapshot, i18n, or parity inconsistencies. Do not report mere WIP unless behavior is inconsistent.
findings: 已读取 existing registry，重点核对 ISS-20260609-001..059；读取 dirty-files.txt 与 fabric-doctor.txt，并用 git status/diff/ls-files 交叉确认。dirty-files.txt 仅列 .workflow/issues/issues.jsonl，当前 packages/cli 与 packages/shared 范围内无未提交 modified/staged/untracked product 文件，因此没有可落到 product file:line 的新增 contract/schema/snapshot/i18n/parity inconsistency。

title: Doctor Warning Issue Sweep
description: Use fabric-doctor.txt. Turn live doctor warnings into issue candidates only when they represent deterministic code/config inconsistency not already registered. Avoid pure local maintenance suggestions without code/root cause.
findings: 已扫 existing registry（含 ISS-20260609-001..059）、fabric-doctor.txt、dirty-files、packages/docs/.claude/.codex 范围。新增 3 个低危：SKILL description lint 的 CRLF false positive、retired archive-hint orphan hook 造成不可修复 parity warning、fabric-archive canonical SKILL 超过 doctor token budget。跳过 events ledger/KB summary/store scope 等本机维护或已登记根因。
issues_found: [{"title":"Doctor skill description lint treats CRLF SKILL frontmatter as missing","severity":"low","description":"fabric-doctor reports fabric-archive/fabric-review/fabric-import as missing YAML frontmatter even though the installed and packaged SKILL.md files start with frontmatter bytes `---\\r\\nname:`. The lint uses an LF-only regex, unlike neighboring doctor frontmatter parsers that accept `\\r?\\n`, so Windows-normalized CRLF skill files become deterministic false positives for skill_description_quality.","location":"packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts:2646; packages/cli/templates/skills/fabric-archive/SKILL.md:1","fix_direction":"Change inspectSkillDescription to use the shared CRLF/BOM-tolerant frontmatter regex (`/^(?:\\uFEFF)?---\\r?\\n([\\s\\S]*?)\\r?\\n---/u`) and add CRLF SKILL.md regression coverage for description lint.","affected_components":["fabric doctor","skill_description_quality","installed Fabric skills","Windows/CRLF skill files"],"tags":["doctor-warning-issues","crlf","skill-description","false-positive"]},{"title":"Retired archive-hint hook files keep doctor hook parity warnings alive","severity":"low","description":"hooks_content_drift scans every `.cjs` basename present in client hook directories and compares same-name files across clients. The repo still has retired `.claude/hooks/archive-hint.cjs` and `.codex/hooks/archive-hint.cjs` with different contents, while the install path only sweeps archive-hint config entries and validates current fabric-hint hooks. Doctor therefore warns on an unconfigured legacy file, and its suggested `fabric install` remediation may not clear the warning.","location":"packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts:4017; packages/cli/src/install/skills-and-hooks.ts:1595; .claude/hooks/archive-hint.cjs:1; .codex/hooks/archive-hint.cjs:1","fix_direction":"Either delete legacy archive-hint.cjs files during install/uninstall/migration, or make hooks_content_drift ignore retired basenames unless they are still referenced by client hook config. Add a regression where stale archive-hint files remain on disk but no config points to them.","affected_components":["fabric doctor","hooks_content_drift","fabric install hook migration",".claude/.codex hook directories"],"tags":["doctor-warning-issues","hook-drift","legacy-hook","migration-tail"]},{"title":"Canonical fabric-archive SKILL exceeds the doctor hot-path token budget","severity":"low","description":"fabric-doctor reports fabric-archive at 5721 estimated tokens, and the packaged template plus installed copies are 17162 chars / 171 lines. This is not just local state: packages/cli/templates/skills/fabric-archive/SKILL.md ships the over-budget hot-path body, so fresh installs inherit the warning and extra model-load cost.","location":"packages/cli/templates/skills/fabric-archive/SKILL.md:1; .claude/skills/fabric-archive/SKILL.md:1; .codex/skills/fabric-archive/SKILL.md:1","fix_direction":"Move detailed phase mechanics, examples, and long MUST/NEVER catalogues into ref/ files and leave SKILL.md with the trigger gate plus concise phase index. Keep token-budget CI/doctor coverage on the canonical template, not only installed copies.","affected_components":["fabric-archive skill","packages/cli/templates/skills","skill_token_budget_exceeded","progressive disclosure"],"tags":["doctor-warning-issues","skill-token-budget","progressive-disclosure","template-size"]}]

title: Installed vs Template Drift Sweep
description: Compare installed .claude/.codex hooks/skills/managed blocks against templates and tests for drift. Focus on deterministic installed-output mismatch or missing gate.
findings: 扫过 existing registry ISS-20260609-001..059、doctor 证据、.claude/.codex hooks/skills、hook config 模板、bootstrap canonical 与安装测试。新增 3 个未登记漂移：AGENTS managed block 仍是旧 agents.meta 文案；遗留 archive-hint.cjs 文件仍安装在两端且内容互相漂移；当前 Codex PreToolUse 顺序与模板相反而测试只做包含断言。
issues_found: [{"title":"Root AGENTS.md managed block still carries retired agents.meta bootstrap text","severity":"medium","description":"The live root AGENTS.md fabric:bootstrap block is not byte-aligned with BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL: it still tells developers not to edit .fabric/agents.meta.json and lists that file as a DON'T item, while the canonical template now says Fabric-managed derived state and no longer names agents.meta. fabric-doctor.txt also reports bootstrap_snapshot_drift, so the installed AI policy surface can keep teaching retired project-local state semantics until install/doctor rewrites it.","location":"AGENTS.md:19; AGENTS.md:29; AGENTS.md:42; packages/shared/src/templates/bootstrap-canonical.ts:69; packages/shared/src/templates/bootstrap-canonical.ts:79","fix_direction":"Run the canonical bootstrap rewrite path (`fabric install` or doctor fix) so AGENTS.md is regenerated from .fabric/AGENTS.md/BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL; keep the live AGENTS.md drift guard in CI so retired agents.meta text cannot remain in the checked-in installed surface.","affected_components":["AGENTS.md","bootstrap managed block","BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL","AI client policy docs"],"tags":["installed-output-drift","bootstrap-drift","agents-meta-retired","live-state"]},{"title":"Legacy archive-hint.cjs files remain under installed hook trees after the fabric-hint rename","severity":"low","description":"The current installed trees still contain .claude/hooks/archive-hint.cjs and .codex/hooks/archive-hint.cjs even though packages/cli/templates/hooks has no archive-hint.cjs source and install now copies fabric-hint.cjs. The install sweep only strips config entries by basename before merge; it does not delete the stale script files, and fabric-doctor.txt reports cross-client content drift for archive-hint.cjs. These orphan scripts preserve retired local knowledge/pending logic and create noisy deterministic drift in installed hook audits.","location":".claude/hooks/archive-hint.cjs:1; .codex/hooks/archive-hint.cjs:1; packages/cli/src/install/skills-and-hooks.ts:1588; packages/cli/src/install/skills-and-hooks.ts:1716; packages/cli/__tests__/integration/install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts:490","fix_direction":"Extend the rc27 cleanup to remove legacy archive-hint.cjs files from .claude/.codex/.cursor hook directories during install, or explicitly exclude retired basenames from hook-content drift checks after config sweep. Add an install regression that seeds archive-hint.cjs files and asserts they are gone after reinstall.","affected_components":["installed hook files","install cleanup","fabric doctor hooks_content_drift","install-skills-and-hooks tests"],"tags":["installed-output-drift","legacy-hook","archive-hint-rename","doctor-drift"]},{"title":"Installed Codex PreToolUse hook order drifts from the codex hook template","severity":"low","description":"The live .codex/hooks.json runs cite-policy-evict.cjs before knowledge-hint-narrow.cjs, while packages/cli/templates/hooks/configs/codex-hooks.json puts knowledge-hint-narrow.cjs first and cite-policy-evict.cjs second. The integration test only asserts that PreToolUse contains knowledge-hint-narrow and the matcher, so an order drift in the installed output can survive even though hook order is part of the generated config contract.","location":".codex/hooks.json:13; .codex/hooks.json:16; .codex/hooks.json:20; packages/cli/templates/hooks/configs/codex-hooks.json:13; packages/cli/templates/hooks/configs/codex-hooks.json:16; packages/cli/templates/hooks/configs/codex-hooks.json:20; packages/cli/__tests__/integration/install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts:206","fix_direction":"Canonicalize Codex fabric-owned hook entries on reinstall and add an exact ordered assertion for events.PreToolUse against the template fragment, while preserving unrelated user hooks outside the fabric-owned sweep.","affected_components":[".codex/hooks.json","codex hook config template","install config merge tests"],"tags":["installed-output-drift","codex-hooks","hook-order","test-gate-drift"]}]

title: Knowledge Store Health Tail Sweep
description: Scan store scope, summary, alias-link, maturity parsing, events/metrics health tail issues beyond registry. Use doctor output plus code evidence.
findings: 已读 registry 并避开 ISS-20260609-001..059 中的 store routing、alias traversal、metric G11 等已登记根因；扫描 doctor 输出、store scope lint、by-alias、events/metrics、maturity/summary 相关代码。新增 2 个 health-tail 问题：visibility_store 校验缺口与 by-alias symlink 不可用时的永久告警。
issues_found: [{"title":"Store scope lint never verifies visibility_store matches the physical holding store","severity":"medium","description":"entryScopeMetadataSchema defines visibility_store as the alias/UUID of the store where an entry physically lives, and write-side code stamps target.alias. lintStoreScopes reads visibility_store but only checks whether it is missing; the subsequent checks cover personal leaks and dangling project refs, not whether visibility_store equals the actual read-set store alias/UUID. A canonical team entry physically in store A with visibility_store: \"B\" can pass doctor scope lint, leaving stale provenance invisible even though recall/citation surfaces depend on store provenance.","location":"packages/server/src/services/doctor-scope-lint.ts:130","fix_direction":"Add a visibility_store_mismatch violation comparing the parsed visibility_store against the current StoreCtx alias and/or uuid, include it in createScopeLintCheck breakdown/remediation, and add a regression where an entry in the team store declares another store alias.","affected_components":["store scope lint","fabric doctor","store provenance metadata","cross-store recall/citation diagnostics"],"tags":["knowledge-store-health-tail","store-scope-lint","visibility-store","provenance","doctor-gap"]},{"title":"by-alias best-effort symlink failures turn into permanent doctor drift warnings","severity":"low","description":"syncStoreAliasLinks creates ~/.fabric/stores/by-alias before attempting symlinkSync and swallows symlink failures as best-effort errors. detectAliasLinkDrift suppresses drift only when the by-alias directory does not exist; once doctor --fix or store mutation has created the directory on a platform/account that cannot create symlinks, missing links are reported forever with a remediation that cannot succeed. The test suite also returns early when sync.errors includes the alias, so this failure mode is not asserted.","location":"packages/cli/src/store/store-ops.ts:101","fix_direction":"Persist or infer a symlink-unsupported state and make detectAliasLinkDrift return [] when sync cannot create links, or avoid creating by-alias until at least one link succeeds. Add a test that forces syncStoreAliasLinks to error and then asserts doctor/detectAliasLinkDrift stays quiet or reports an actionable unsupported-state message instead of repairable drift.","affected_components":["fabric doctor store health","by-alias readability links","Windows/unprivileged filesystem support","store diagnostics"],"tags":["knowledge-store-health-tail","by-alias","doctor-noise","windows","best-effort-drift"]}]

title: Registry Artifact Integrity Sweep
description: Validate generated discovery artifacts, issues.jsonl schema/source/ID ordering/history consistency after multiple rounds. Avoid duplicate source-mix/history issues already known.
findings: 已读 existing registry 并排除 ISS-20260608-047 的 source-mix 与 ISS-20260609-050 的 history root cause；扫描 .workflow/issues JSON/JSONL、discovery-state/discovery-issues、packages 范围引用。新增 1 个 artifact integrity 问题：discovery index 未登记任何已完成 DBP 多轮发现。dirty-files.txt/fabric-doctor.txt 未在工作树找到。
issues_found: [{"title":"Discovery catalog index omits every completed DBP discovery run","severity":"medium","description":".workflow/issues/discoveries/index.json still contains only the old DSC-20260421-184152 entry, while the discoveries directory contains 15 completed DBP-* runs with discovery-state.json and discovery-issues.jsonl up through ISS-20260609-059. Consumers that rely on the catalog index cannot discover or validate the generated multi-round artifacts, so registry artifact integrity checks silently miss the live DBP history.","location":".workflow/issues/discoveries/index.json:2","fix_direction":"Update the discovery completion/write path to append or upsert DBP runs into index.json, or replace the static index with a derived directory scan. Add an integrity check that every completed discovery-state.json has a corresponding index entry and that current in-progress runs are explicitly excluded.","affected_components":[".workflow/issues/discoveries/index.json","discovery artifact catalog","issue discovery registry tooling"],"tags":["registry-artifact-integrity","discovery-index","artifact-drift"]}]
